mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one trading week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where the unemployment threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery that sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in retail sales and housing starts fails to produce a positive mean forward return over 60 days in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during an economic expansion signals supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins and force negative S&P 500 adjustments over the following month.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines during expansions do not predict negative 20-day forward returns with a t-statistic below -1.5.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI decline below 2.0 percent year-over-year removes inflationary pressure concerns, prompting immediate equity market rallies and positive S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain negative or flat in 70% of observations when CPI growth drops below the 2.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate consumer spending capacity, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling real GDP and rising retail sales indicates unsustainable consumption financed by debt, which triggers a correction in equity valuations and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The GDP-retail sales divergence fails to predict negative 20-day returns with an out-of-sample accuracy exceeding 55%.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in industrial production during a recessionary trough signals the onset of economic recovery, prompting front-running by institutional investors and positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Industrial production accelerations during recessions do not yield statistically significant positive 5-day forward returns (p > 0.05).
